config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama agar kepentingan masyarakat tidak berubah jadi alasan untuk menekan orang yang lemah atau minoritas. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi seharusnya dicapai dengan menghormati hak dasar tiap orang, bukan dengan mengorbankannya. Kalau hak individu terlalu mudah dikalahkan, yang sering terjadi justru penyalahgunaan kuasa atas nama “kepentingan umum”.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should automatically take priority over the individual, because in the United States that idea has too often been used to justify abuse, conformity, and trampling on minorities. Public safety and the common good matter, but they should be limited by strong individual rights, especially when the majority wants something convenient or popular. If the rights of one person can be ignored whenever society claims a need, then those rights are not real protections at all. To me, the burden should be on society to prove a serious necessity before overriding individual liberty, not the other way around.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU, karena menurut saya kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh otomatis berada di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman di Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” sering dipakai untuk membenarkan penggusuran, pembungkaman, atau perlakuan tidak adil terhadap kelompok kecil, jadi saya sangat waspada kalau hak individu dibuat mudah dikalahkan. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai lewat pembatasan yang ketat, transparan, dan benar-benar perlu, bukan sekadar karena mayoritas merasa lebih nyaman. Kalau hak orang per orang tidak dilindungi, yang paling mudah dirugikan biasanya justru warga yang paling lemah.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe the interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual, because in the United States that idea has repeatedly been used to excuse abuse, majority rule without restraint, and discrimination against unpopular groups. Public safety and the common good are real, but they only stay legitimate when they are constrained by strong rights that cannot be waved away for convenience. I also do not buy the argument that society is always the more important side, because once you make individual rights conditional on collective approval, they stop functioning as rights and become permissions. A just society should protect people first and only override liberty in narrow, truly necessary cases with clear limits.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu. Di Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan penggusuran, pembungkaman, atau perlakuan tidak adil, jadi bagi saya hak individu harus menjadi pagar utama. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi justru sah kalau dicapai lewat aturan yang transparan, proporsional, dan benar-benar perlu, bukan lewat persetujuan mayoritas semata. Kalau hak orang per orang mudah dikalahkan, yang paling cepat jadi korban biasanya warga yang paling lemah.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over individual rights, because in the United States that idea has too often been a cover for majority rule, discrimination, and government overreach. I understand the point about public safety and the common good, but those goals are only legitimate when they are tightly limited and cannot be used as a blank check against unpopular people. Once rights become conditional on what society wants at the moment, they are no longer real rights, just privileges that can be revoked. A fair society should put the burden on society to justify any override, and that justification should have to be narrow, necessary, and temporary.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
